title: 2026 Wisconsin Governor Election
description: Wisconsin’s 2026 gubernatorial election is an open-seat race after Democratic Gov. Tony Evers said he would not seek reelection. The contest is unfolding in a closely divided battleground state, with the governor’s office likely to influence taxes, schools, health care, abortion policy, labor issues, energy costs, and election administration. Both parties have crowded or developing primary fields, and early polling shows many voters are still undecided and unfamiliar with the candidates. The race is expected to hinge on which party can define the stakes and consolidate support before the August primary and November general election.

candidates.name: Sara Rodriguez
candidates.summary: Sara Rodriguez is Wisconsin’s lieutenant governor and a former state Assembly member from suburban Milwaukee. A nurse and public health professional, she entered the 2026 governor’s race as a Democrat focused on lowering costs, expanding health care access, and funding public services.

summary_sources.title: About Sara Rodriguez | Leadership for Wisconsin
About Lieutenant Governor Sara Rodriguez
Sara Rodriguez - Ballotpedia

career_history.title: Founder
career_history.description: Founded a health care consulting firm.

top_donors.name: North Central State Regional Council Of Carpenters
top_donors.organization: North Central State Regional Council Of Carpenters

Healthcare.stance: Rodriguez says she would lower health care costs and guarantee affordable, reliable coverage for Wisconsin residents. Her campaign has also promoted a Wisconsin-governed public option, stronger BadgerCare, postpartum coverage, mental health parity, and protections for pre-existing conditions and reproductive health care.

sources.title: Issues — Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin
Make Health Care Less Expensive
Sara Rodriguez lays out plan for a public option in health care

Economy.stance: Rodriguez says she would lower costs for families by cutting property taxes, making child care more affordable, and helping more Wisconsinites buy homes. Her campaign also emphasizes wages and long-term affordability for working families.

sources.title: Issues — Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin
Make Child Care Affordable for Families

Climate/Energy.stance: Rodriguez says she would lower utility costs and energy bills while protecting Wisconsin’s air, water, and land. She has also said large energy users such as data centers should cover their own energy costs and that projects should include local input and renewable energy commitments.

sources.title: Issues — Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin
Sara Rodriguez on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Reproductive Rights.stance: Rodriguez says she would protect reproductive freedom and ensure people can make their own health care decisions. Her campaign says it would defend abortion rights and access to contraception and fertility care.

sources.title: Issues — Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin
Defend Reproductive Rights

Immigration.stance: Rodriguez says she would stand up to federal immigration enforcement she views as reckless and protect Wisconsin communities. Her campaign has also called for limits on enforcement at sensitive locations such as schools, hospitals, courthouses, and places of worship.

sources.title: Issues — Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin
Rodriguez calls for bans on immigration enforcements at specific locations

Guns & Safety.stance: Rodriguez’s campaign says she would keep communities safe and build a justice system that works, but it does not lay out a detailed firearms policy on the campaign site reviewed. Public materials emphasize public safety, fairness, and second chances.

sources.title: Issues — Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin

Social Justice.stance: Rodriguez says she would reform the criminal justice system so it keeps communities safe, treats people fairly, and gives people a second chance. Her campaign also says it would defend civil rights and protect Wisconsin from federal overreach affecting health care, schools, voting rights, and communities.

sources.title: Issues — Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin

Education.stance: Rodriguez says she would fully fund public schools and keep property taxes from rising because of school costs. Her campaign also says it would support child care and keep kids safe online as part of a broader family-support agenda.

sources.title: Issues — Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin
Make Child Care Affordable for Families

Tech & AI.stance: Rodriguez says she would make AI work for Wisconsin workers rather than only for tech companies. Her campaign also says it would protect kids online by holding Big Tech accountable and limiting harmful design and advertising practices.

sources.title: Issues — Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin
Rodriguez campaign releases plan to keep Wisconsin kids safe online and hold Big Tech accountable

Election Reform.stance: Rodriguez has supported automatic voter registration and previously authored AVR legislation in the Wisconsin Assembly. Her campaign also says it would protect voting rights from federal overreach.

sources.title: Who is Sara Rodriguez, the first Democrat to jump into Wisconsin governor's race?
Issues — Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin

Local Issues.stance: Rodriguez says she would support local communities by lowering property taxes, making housing more affordable, and requiring local input on major energy projects. Her campaign also emphasizes clean air and water protections and community-level decision-making.

sources.title: Issues — Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin
Sara Rodriguez on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

candidates.donor_summary: Sara Rodriguez’s publicly available campaign-finance data shows a mix of individual donors, PACs, and entity contributions, with the largest publicly accessible state-level totals coming from her Wisconsin campaign filings rather than the unrelated 2022 Tony Evers OpenSecrets ticket page. OpenSecrets’ Evers page does list Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin as one contributor to Evers’ 2022 committee, but that source is ticket-level data and should not be treated as Rodriguez’s own donor list.

links.title: Sara Rodriguez on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
Rodriguez campaign: Announces over $5 million raised in race for Wisconsin governor
Sunshine: Wisconsin Campaign Finance reports search
Wisconsin Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez enters 2026 governor race
Tony Evers - OpenSecrets Top Contributors (2022 ticket data including Sara Rodriguez)
Sara Rodriguez on the 2026 Primary for Governor of Wisconsin
Sara Rodriguez - Wisconsin Candidate - Transparency USA

candidates.voting_summary: Sara Rodriguez is an incumbent Wisconsin lieutenant governor, not a legislator, so there is no legislative voting record to report. Her comparable governing record is executive: she has served in the Evers administration since 2023, but I did not find a public roll-call voting record for her in the sources reviewed. Sources: https://ballotpedia.org/Sara_Rodriguez ; https://evers.wi.gov/Pages/Newsroom/About_Mandela.aspx

candidates.name: Tom Tiffany
candidates.summary: Tom Tiffany is a Republican U.S. representative from northern Wisconsin and a candidate for governor in 2026. He previously served in the Wisconsin Legislature and has centered his campaign on taxes, education, immigration enforcement, energy costs, and conservative social policy.

summary_sources.title: Thomas Tiffany | Congress.gov
Tom's Solutions - Tom Tiffany for Governor
Tom Tiffany - Ballotpedia

source.title: H.R.3937 - 119th Congress (2025-2026): Wabeno Economic Development Act
Rep. Thomas Tiffany's 2024 Report Card - GovTrack

Healthcare.stance: Tiffany says he wants to lower healthcare costs by cutting regulations and relying on market-based reforms rather than expanding government programs. His campaign says it would make healthcare more affordable through deregulation, and reporting indicates he has opposed expanding ACA subsidies.

sources.title: Tom's Solutions - Tom Tiffany for Governor
A Better Wisconsin Together: Tom Tiffany votes to take healthcare away from 60,000 Wisconsinites

Economy.stance: Tiffany says he would freeze property taxes, repeal Gov. Evers’ proposed 400-year tax increase, end state income taxes on tips and overtime, and cut regulations to reduce costs for families and businesses. He also says he would pursue lower energy costs as part of his economic agenda.

sources.title: Tom's Solutions - Tom Tiffany for Governor
Tom Tiffany plans on ending state income taxes on tips, overtime in Wausau visit
Tiffany campaign: Announces plan in Wausau to eliminate state income taxes on tips and overtime

Climate/Energy.stance: Tiffany says he would lower energy costs by expanding reliable baseload power and opposing wind and solar projects on farmland. He also supports ending vehicle emissions testing in southeast Wisconsin.

sources.title: Tom's Solutions - Tom Tiffany for Governor
Should Wisconsin get rid of car emissions tests? Tom Tiffany says yes

Reproductive Rights.stance: Tiffany has said he would uphold Wisconsin’s current abortion law if elected governor.

sources.title: Abortion laws and Wisconsin's 2026 candidates for governor
What Bill Berrien, Josh Schoemann and Tom Tiffany say about abortion

Immigration.stance: Tiffany supports aggressive immigration enforcement, including working with federal authorities such as ICE, and his campaign says it would ban sanctuary cities in Wisconsin.

sources.title: Tom's Solutions - Tom Tiffany for Governor
US Rep. Tom Tiffany, GOP candidate for governor, blames Democrats for Minnesota shooting

Guns & Safety.stance: Tiffany strongly supports Second Amendment rights and has said concealed carry should be allowed for permit holders, including at protests, while emphasizing responsible and lawful carry.

sources.title: US Rep. Tom Tiffany, GOP candidate for governor, blames Democrats for Minnesota shooting
Wisconsin Democrats use gun rights to target GOP candidate Tom Tiffany

Foreign Policy.stance: Tiffany has criticized what he describes as a globalist foreign-policy approach and has supported cutting foreign aid and reducing the State Department budget.

sources.title: The Tiffany Telegram: January 16, 2026 | Representative Tom Tiffany
US Rep. Tom Tiffany on the border, inflation, foreign policy

Social Justice.stance: Tiffany’s campaign messaging is socially conservative, including opposition to policies it says conflict with its views on gender identity in schools and support for girls’ sports and locker rooms being for girls.

sources.title: Tom's Solutions - Tom Tiffany for Governor
Tom Tiffany, Rob Kreibich Call on New Richmond Schools to Reverse Policy Allowing Males in Girls’ Bathrooms

Education.stance: Tiffany says he would increase education funding while requiring accountability, raise teacher pay, reward successful schools and administrators, and emphasize early literacy and classroom instruction.

sources.title: Tom's Solutions - Tom Tiffany for Governor
Tom Tiffany on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Tech & AI.stance: Tiffany says he would end subsidies for data centers in Wisconsin and oppose government incentives for AI and data-center development.

sources.title: Where the candidates stand on data center construction
Tiffany vows to end subsidies for data centers in Wisconsin
Tom Tiffany Instagram post: I will end all subsidies for data centers in Wisconsin

Election Reform.stance: Tiffany has signaled opposition to expanding mail-in voting and says it should not be widespread, with a limited exception for military voters.

sources.title: Governor candidate Tom Tiffany sends mixed signals on mail-in voting
Tom Tiffany, regular absentee voter, says 'I don't believe we should be doing mail-in voting'

Local Issues.stance: Tiffany says local governments should be well-funded, especially for roads and bridges, and he favors local control on projects like mining and housing-related zoning decisions.

sources.title: Tom Tiffany on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
Tom's Solutions - Tom Tiffany for Governor

candidates.donor_summary: Tom Tiffany’s OpenSecrets profile shows federal campaign-finance data for his congressional committee, and Wisconsin reporting said he had raised about $2 million in the governor’s race. The public sources I found do not provide a clean industry breakdown in the search snippets, but OpenSecrets is the best full donor reference for his federal fundraising history and individual donors. Sources: https://www.opensecrets.org/profiles/tom-tiffany/us_congress/summary?mpid=1109891 ; https://wisconsinexaminer.com/2026/01/16/crowley-and-tiffany-lead-fundraising-in-governors-race/

candidates.voting_summary: Tom Tiffany is an incumbent U.S. representative, so he has a legislative voting record. GovTrack and Vote Smart both track his votes; his record is strongly aligned with the Republican caucus, and his 2024 GovTrack report card shows a conservative voting pattern. Sources: https://www.govtrack.us/congress/members/thomas_tiffany/456791/report-card/2024 ; https://justfacts.votesmart.org/candidate/key-votes/51831/tom-tiffany

candidates.name: David D. King
candidates.summary: David D. King is a Milwaukee-based pastor and community leader running as an independent for Wisconsin governor. His campaign says he is focused on education, health care, infrastructure, public safety, and economic opportunity, and it highlights his work with community outreach and transitional support programs.

summary_sources.title: Meet David King | King For Governor
My Platform | King For Governor
David King - PBS Wisconsin

career_history.title: Founder
career_history.description: Founded a community organization that has assisted over 2,000 individuals facing homelessness, addiction, or prior convictions by helping them find employment and transportation.

career_history.title: Founder
career_history.description: Established to support small businesses and ministries by offering networking opportunities and access to financial instruments.

Healthcare.stance: King supports a Wisconsin Whole Health Plan that would integrate traditional medicine with chiropractic and other holistic services, including acupuncture, naturopathy, massage therapy, and nutrition counseling. The plan also calls for lower out-of-pocket costs, preventive health programs, and new integrative clinics in underserved areas.

sources.title: My Platform | King For Governor

Economy.stance: King’s economic plan emphasizes expanding manufacturing and innovation, including AI and research partnerships, while cutting red tape for small businesses and giving Wisconsin firms first shot at state contracts. He also calls for workforce training, apprenticeships, childcare solutions, support for agriculture and rural areas, and fiscal restraint.

sources.title: My Platform | King For Governor

Climate/Energy.stance: King says Wisconsin should strengthen the grid, support clean energy, and protect against outages while also prioritizing roads, bridges, broadband, and clean water infrastructure. His platform frames energy policy as part of a broader infrastructure agenda.

sources.title: My Platform | King For Governor

Reproductive Rights.stance: No public position found in the campaign materials reviewed. The campaign website pages reviewed do not state a position on abortion or reproductive rights.

sources.title: My Platform | King For Governor
Meet David King | King For Governor

Immigration.stance: No public position found in the campaign materials reviewed. The campaign pages reviewed do not include an immigration-specific policy statement.

sources.title: My Platform | King For Governor
Meet David King | King For Governor

Guns & Safety.stance: King’s Wisconsin Safe Communities Plan says he would be tough on violent crime while expanding community policing, neighborhood officers, youth jobs, mentoring, and efforts against drug trafficking and fentanyl. The plan also sets public year-one targets for reducing shootings, homicides, overdose deaths, and reckless-driving fatalities.

sources.title: My Platform | King For Governor

Foreign Policy.stance: No public position found in the campaign materials reviewed. The campaign website focuses on Wisconsin state issues and does not present a foreign-policy platform.

sources.title: My Platform | King For Governor
Meet David King | King For Governor

Social Justice.stance: King presents his campaign as focused on community uplift, unity, and service, highlighting ministry and civic work that has assisted people facing homelessness, addiction, and reentry challenges. His messaging emphasizes putting people before politics and bringing Wisconsinites together.

sources.title: Meet David King | King For Governor
My Platform | King For Governor

Education.stance: King’s education plan calls for reading by third grade, math mastery by eighth grade, higher pay pathways and less paperwork for teachers, more mental health support in schools, paid youth apprenticeships and dual-credit opportunities, and fairer school funding.

sources.title: My Platform | King For Governor

Tech & AI.stance: King’s platform says Wisconsin should grow manufacturing and innovation, including AI and research partnerships, but it does not lay out a detailed standalone technology or AI policy.

sources.title: My Platform | King For Governor
Meet David King | King For Governor

Local Issues.stance: King’s local-issues agenda centers on education, infrastructure, holistic health care, public safety, and economic development. His platform also emphasizes broadband access, clean water, reliable energy, support for farms and small businesses, and community revitalization.

sources.title: My Platform | King For Governor
Issues | King For Governor

candidates.donor_summary: David D. King’s public finance footprint appears to be limited and mostly state-level. The Wisconsin Transparency USA page exists, and his campaign donation page is public, but I did not find a reliable public total raised or a detailed industry breakdown in the sources reviewed. Sources: https://www.transparencyusa.org/wi/candidate/david-king ; https://www.kingforgovernor.com/donations

Sources: https://ballotpedia.org/David_King_(Wisconsin) ; https://pbswisconsin.org/candidate/david-king/

candidates.name: Francesca Hong
candidates.summary: Francesca Hong is a Democratic member of the Wisconsin State Assembly representing Madison’s 76th District and a candidate for Wisconsin governor in 2026. Her background includes work as a chef, restaurant worker, community organizer, and single mother, and her campaign says it is focused on affordability, public services, and working families.

summary_sources.title: Meet Francesca Hong - Wisconsin State Legislature
Francesca Hong For Governor | We Make Better Possible
Francesca Hong - Ballotpedia

career_history.title: State Representative
career_history.description: Represents Wisconsin Assembly District 76 in Madison.

candidates.donor_summary: Francesca Hong’s OpenSecrets profile shows modest state-level fundraising in prior cycles, with one OpenSecrets summary listing about $29,741 in contributions and another showing small-item donors such as the Public Fund ($550), Moving Wisconsin Forward ($300), and the National Association of Realtors ($300). Her 2026 governor campaign is described in reporting as grassroots-oriented, but the public finance data I found suggests a relatively small fundraising base compared with the major statewide candidates. Sources: https://www.opensecrets.org/officeholders/francesca-hong/summary?cycle=2022&id=50058291 ; https://www.opensecrets.org/officeholders/francesca-hong/summary?cycle=2021&id=50058291 ; https://urbanmilwaukee.com/pressrelease/senator-kelda-roys-announces-378k-raised-from-over-82-wisconsin-donors/
candidates.voting_summary: Francesca Hong is an incumbent Wisconsin state assembly member, so she does have a legislative voting record. Vote Smart and Ballotpedia identify her as a current Assembly member, and her public record is consistent with a Democratic caucus alignment; however, I did not find a single authoritative roll-call summary in the sources reviewed. Sources: https://justfacts.votesmart.org/candidate/193953/francesca-hong ; https://ballotpedia.org/Francesca_Hong

links.title: Roll Call: WI AB651 | 2025-2026 | Regular Session
Francesca Hong For Governor | We Make Better Possible
State Rep. Francesca Hong enters 2026 race for Wisconsin governor
Meet Francesca Hong - Wisconsin State Legislature

Healthcare.stance: Hong supports expanding Medicaid and establishing a strong public option, cracking down on claim denials, and making large hospital corporations pay more while helping small hospitals and community clinics stay stable. In a PBS Wisconsin interview, she also said abortion is health care and that access should be equitable.

sources.title: Francesca Hong campaign site
PBS Wisconsin - Francesca Hong on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Economy.stance: Hong’s economic platform centers on affordability for working families, including universal childcare, universal paid leave, fully funded public schools, and tax fairness that closes loopholes and asks more of high earners and large corporations. She also supports housing affordability measures and a public bank to help with lending for housing and small businesses.

sources.title: Francesca Hong campaign site
PBS Wisconsin - Francesca Hong on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Climate/Energy.stance: Hong supports stronger limits on subsidies for large data centers and says Wisconsin should not hand out subsidies to corporations that raise costs for ratepayers or harm the environment. She has also said she is opposed to harmful mining and drilling projects and wants communities protected from environmental damage.

sources.title: PBS Wisconsin - Francesca Hong on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
Francesca Hong campaign site

Reproductive Rights.stance: Hong supports repealing Wisconsin’s 1849 abortion ban and says abortion is health care that should be accessible equitably. She has also backed the Reproductive Freedom Act and opposes restrictions that interfere with reproductive health care.

sources.title: PBS Wisconsin - Francesca Hong on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
Sen. Roys, Rep. Hong introduce Reproductive Freedom Act

Immigration.stance: Hong supports stronger protections for immigrant communities, including limiting ICE cooperation, protecting due process, and expanding access to state IDs and in-state tuition for DACA recipients. Her campaign says Wisconsin should use state authority to protect residents regardless of immigration status.

sources.title: Francesca Hong campaign site
Francesca Hong campaign policy page

Guns & Safety.stance: Hong supports universal gun safety measures such as universal background checks, red flag laws, and safe-storage requirements. Her public comments frame these policies as child-safety and violence-prevention measures.

sources.title: PBS Wisconsin - Francesca Hong on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
PBS - Francesca Hong on the 2026 primary for governor of Wisconsin

Social Justice.stance: Hong’s platform emphasizes economic dignity and equity, including renter protections, right to counsel in eviction cases, LGBTQ-inclusive health care, immigrant protections, and criminal-justice reforms that expand due process. She frames these policies as part of a broader effort to reduce racial, gender, and class disparities.

sources.title: Francesca Hong campaign policy page
Francesca Hong campaign site

Education.stance: Hong supports fully funding public schools, increasing special education reimbursement, and shifting school funding away from property taxes. She opposes voucher programs and says public dollars should go to public schools, universities, and technical colleges.

sources.title: Francesca Hong campaign policy page
PBS Wisconsin - Francesca Hong on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Local Issues.stance: Hong supports a moratorium on new data centers until Wisconsin adopts statewide standards, and she wants local communities to have stronger input on energy, environmental, and tax impacts. She also backs local funding tools such as a local option income tax and community-based financing for housing and public services.

sources.title: Francesca Hong campaign policy page
Wisconsin Examiner - data center development

Tech & AI.stance: Hong says Wisconsin should pause new AI data-center construction until the state has rules to protect electricity rates, the environment, and local communities. She opposes tax breaks for data centers and says any approved projects should provide long-term public benefit and use union labor.

sources.title: Francesca Hong campaign site
Wisconsin Examiner - data center development

Election Reform.stance: Hong has opposed legislation she says would make voting and campaign participation harder, including a 2025 bill on absentee ballots and a 2025 bill on online political donations. Her public record suggests support for easier access to voting and skepticism of restrictive election measures.

sources.title: Vote Smart - Francesca Hong voting record

candidates.name: Mandela Barnes
candidates.summary: Mandela Barnes is a Democratic candidate for Wisconsin governor and a former Wisconsin lieutenant governor. He served as Wisconsin's 45th lieutenant governor from 2019 to 2023 and is running in the open 2026 governor's race.

summary_sources.title: Mandela Barnes for Wisconsin Governor
About Lieutenant Governor Mandela Barnes
Mandela Barnes launches 2026 campaign for Wisconsin governor

candidates.donor_summary: Mandela Barnes has substantial prior fundraising history, including an OpenSecrets record showing $512,756 in reported payments for his 2024 campaign vendor activity and donor pages listing contributors from his 2021 officeholder cycle. Wisconsin reporting also said he aims to raise $50 million for the governor’s race, indicating a large-scale statewide fundraising operation, though the public donor mix in the search results was not fully itemized. Sources: https://www.opensecrets.org/campaign-expenditures/vendor?vendor=Mandela+Barnes+for+Wisconsin ; https://www.opensecrets.org/officeholders/mandela-barnes/contributors?cycle=2021&id=13735954&recs=100 ; https://wisconsinexaminer.com/2025/12/09/mandela-barnes-aims-to-raise-50-million-in-governors-race/
candidates.voting_summary: Mandela Barnes is a former Wisconsin lieutenant governor, not a legislator, so there is no legislative voting record to report. His comparable governing record is executive: he served as Wisconsin’s 45th lieutenant governor and participated in the Evers administration, but I did not find a public roll-call voting record for him. Sources: https://evers.wi.gov/Pages/Newsroom/About_Mandela.aspx ; https://ballotpedia.org/Mandela_Barnes

links.title: Vendor/Recipient: Mandela Barnes for Wisconsin - OpenSecrets
Mandela Barnes enters 2026 race for Wisconsin governor
Mandela Barnes aims to raise $50 million in governor's race

Healthcare.stance: Barnes says his first priority as governor would be to expand BadgerCare right away, including in a special session if necessary, and he said he would refuse to sign a state budget that does not include expansion. He frames the issue as a way to reduce health care costs and expand access for Wisconsin families.

sources.title: Mandela Barnes on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
The Wisconsin Way — Mandela Barnes for Wisconsin

Climate/Energy.stance: Barnes says Wisconsin should invest in more renewable energy, including wind, solar, and geothermal, and he wants the Public Service Commission to keep costs low for ratepayers. He also says large projects such as data centers should not raise utility rates for customers and should include community benefits agreements and local input.

sources.title: Mandela Barnes on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
The Wisconsin Way — Mandela Barnes for Wisconsin

Guns & Safety.stance: Barnes supports red flag laws, universal background checks, and an assault weapons ban. His campaign says these measures are part of a broader public-safety approach while preserving lawful gun ownership.

sources.title: The Wisconsin Way — Mandela Barnes for Wisconsin
Mandela Barnes - Giffords endorsement
Mandela Barnes on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Social Justice.stance: Barnes says he wants to represent Wisconsin residents rather than a party label and emphasizes civil rights, voting access, and broader community input in state decision-making. He also frames public services and education policy around helping people who face unequal starting conditions.

sources.title: Mandela Barnes on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Education.stance: Barnes says Wisconsin should fully meet its public-school funding pledge, invest in the whole child, and ensure public funding comes with accountability. He also supports stronger support for students’ health, housing, and school resources, along with public accountability for voucher-funded schools.

sources.title: Mandela Barnes on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Tech & AI.stance: Barnes says data centers should face community benefits agreements, local hiring expectations, and protections against higher utility rates for residents. He also wants community input prioritized and says water-quality concerns should be addressed before projects move forward.

sources.title: Mandela Barnes on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
Mandela Barnes talks rising costs, data centers

Election Reform.stance: Barnes says he has worked with bipartisan groups and election officials to strengthen confidence in the electoral process and support democracy reform. He favors changes that improve election integrity and says he is willing to work across party lines on the issue.

sources.title: Mandela Barnes on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Local Issues.stance: Barnes says state government should help municipalities secure resources for basic services and that local leaders should have a meaningful role in decisions affecting their communities. He also ties local affordability concerns to utility rates, housing costs, and data-center siting.

sources.title: Mandela Barnes on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
Barnes' utility freeze plan is legally questionable

Economy.stance: Barnes emphasizes lowering household costs, including housing and utility bills, and says state policy should focus on affordability for Wisconsin families. He also argues for local fiscal solutions that help municipalities maintain services and for public investments that support working people.

sources.title: Mandela Barnes on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
Mandela Barnes pledges to freeze utility rates if elected governor

Reproductive Rights.stance: Barnes said he would like to see abortion rights proactively protected in Wisconsin and return the state to a pre-Dobbs framework. He described abortion as a decision best made between a woman and her health care provider.

sources.title: Abortion laws and Wisconsin's 2026 candidates for governor
Where Wisconsin's candidates for governor stand on ICE cooperation

candidates.name: Kelda Roys
candidates.summary: Kelda Roys is a Democratic member of the Wisconsin State Senate representing District 26 and a candidate for governor in 2026. Before joining the Senate, she served in the Wisconsin State Assembly, led NARAL Pro-Choice Wisconsin, and founded a small business focused on real estate brokerage.

summary_sources.title: About | Senate District 26 - Wisconsin State Legislature
Meet Kelda - Kelda Roys for Governor
Kelda Roys - Ballotpedia

candidates.donor_summary: Kelda Roys has public fundraising data showing a grassroots donor base: one campaign report said she raised $378,000 from more than 82 Wisconsin donors and had more than $335,000 on hand. Earlier OpenSecrets summaries show small-dollar and modest contributions, including the Public Fund ($550) and a few $300 donations, suggesting a donor mix centered on individual supporters rather than a single dominant industry. Sources: https://urbanmilwaukee.com/pressrelease/senator-kelda-roys-announces-378k-raised-from-over-82-wisconsin-donors/ ; https://www.opensecrets.org/officeholders/kelda-roys/summary?cycle=2021&id=50058291
candidates.voting_summary: Kelda Roys is an incumbent Wisconsin state senator, so she has a legislative voting record. Vote Smart and the Wisconsin Legislature identify her as a current senator, and her record is generally aligned with the Democratic caucus; however, I did not find a single authoritative roll-call summary in the sources reviewed. Sources: https://justfacts.votesmart.org/candidate/109053/kelda-roys ; https://legis.wisconsin.gov/senate/26/roys/

links.title: Senator Kelda Roys | Senate District 26 - Wisconsin State Legislature
Kelda Roys on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
Madison state Sen. Kelda Roys joins growing Democratic primary
About | Senate District 26 - Wisconsin State Legislature

Healthcare.stance: Supports expanding affordable coverage through a state public option model, including her KeldaCare proposal that would let Wisconsin residents and businesses buy into the same health plan used by state employees. She also emphasizes lowering prescription drug costs, strengthening the health care workforce, and protecting reproductive and mental health care.

sources.title: Priorities - Kelda Roys for Governor
KeldaCare - Kelda Roys for Governor
Journal Sentinel - Kelda Roys proposes KeldaCare health care platform

Economy.stance: Backs raising wages, including a $15 minimum wage with a path to $20 by 2030, restoring collective bargaining rights, and expanding support for small businesses. Her campaign also says she would pursue paid family and medical leave, affordable childcare, housing affordability, and a state venture fund to help Wisconsin businesses grow.

sources.title: Priorities - Kelda Roys for Governor
Kelda Roys on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
Wisconsin Public Radio - Democrats vying for Wisconsin governor sound off on data centers

Climate/Energy.stance: Supports expanding clean energy and modernizing Wisconsin's energy system, with emphasis on wind, solar, geothermal, and infrastructure that keeps costs down for households. She also wants stronger oversight of data centers so they help pay for grid upgrades and do not shift costs onto ratepayers.

sources.title: Roys Leads on Energy Affordability - Wisconsin Legislature
Wisconsin Public Radio - Democrats vying for Wisconsin governor sound off on data centers, climate accountability
Kelda Roys on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Reproductive Rights.stance: Supports abortion rights, contraception access, and infertility coverage, and says reproductive decisions should remain private and protected in law. Her campaign and legislative biography describe her as a long-time advocate for reproductive freedom.

sources.title: Meet Kelda - Kelda Roys for Governor
Kelda Roys on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
About | Senate District 26 - Wisconsin State Legislature

Immigration.stance: Supports immigration enforcement reforms that limit local participation in ICE raids, increase accountability for ICE conduct, and protect immigrant communities. Her campaign says Wisconsin can have immigration enforcement and secure borders without what it calls lawless ICE violence, and it backs a pathway to citizenship for undocumented immigrants.

sources.title: Priorities - Kelda Roys for Governor
Kelda Roys lays out health care plan, immigration enforcement

Guns & Safety.stance: Supports gun-safety legislation, including a proposal to prohibit firearms on Wisconsin college campuses with limited exceptions for law enforcement, military personnel on duty, and authorized campus programs. The campaign has also tied public safety to broader accountability and community protection measures.

sources.title: Wisconsin Examiner - Democratic lawmakers propose prohibiting concealed carry on college campuses in Wisconsin
Spectrum News 1 - Gun safety on college campuses in Wisconsin
Sen. Roys, Rep. Brown: Announce gun safety legislation

Foreign Policy.stance: No public position found on traditional foreign policy issues. Her campaign materials focus instead on state-level immigration enforcement, federal policy impacts on Wisconsin, and related accountability measures.

sources.title: Priorities - Kelda Roys for Governor

Social Justice.stance: Emphasizes civil rights, voting access, worker protections, and accountability for powerful institutions. Her campaign links social justice to fair wages, affordable health care, public education, housing, and opposition to discriminatory or abusive enforcement practices.

sources.title: Priorities - Kelda Roys for Governor
Kelda Roys on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Education.stance: Supports stronger funding for Wisconsin public schools, ending or reforming voucher programs, and improving teacher retention and professional development. Her campaign also says schools should use evidence-based instruction, including the science of reading, and keep classrooms focused on learning rather than unproven technology.

sources.title: Priorities - Kelda Roys for Governor
WEAC endorses Kelda Roys for Governor
Roys campaign: Our kids deserve the best education in the country

Tech & AI.stance: Supports state guardrails on AI and data-center development, including transparency, community-benefit requirements, and limits on practices she says can harm consumers or raise energy costs. Her campaign also backs protections against AI-driven abuses such as addictive algorithms and insurance denials.

sources.title: Wisconsin Public Radio - Democrats vying for Wisconsin governor sound off on data centers, climate accountability
Priorities - Kelda Roys for Governor
Sen. Kelda Roys Testifies to Protect Wisconsinites from Tech Exploitation

Election Reform.stance: Supports strengthening civic education and voting rights, and reforming campaign finance and redistricting to improve accountability and democracy. Her campaign frames these changes as part of restoring trust in government.

sources.title: Priorities - Kelda Roys for Governor

Local Issues.stance: Supports stronger local control over data-center development, including transparency on environmental and ratepayer impacts and requirements that projects help pay for infrastructure upgrades. Her campaign says local communities should have a meaningful role in decisions that affect land use, utilities, and public costs.

candidates.name: Josh Schoemann
candidates.summary: Josh Schoemann is the Washington County executive and a Republican candidate for Wisconsin governor in 2026. He previously served in county government in Washington County and has emphasized taxes, education, energy, housing, and election administration in his campaign.

summary_sources.title: County Executive - Washington County, WI
Schoemann formally announces run for governor
Washington County Executive Josh Schoemann enters the 2026 Wisconsin governor's race

candidates.donor_summary: Josh Schoemann’s public finance data appears limited in the sources reviewed. I found a Wisconsin Transparency USA candidate page and campaign coverage, but no reliable public total raised or detailed industry breakdown in the search results; the best finance reference is the state campaign finance page. Sources: https://www.transparencyusa.org/wi/candidate/josh-schoemann ; https://campaignfinance.wi.gov/browse-data/reports?office=%5B5%5D
candidates.voting_summary: Josh Schoemann is a county executive, not a legislator, so there is no legislative voting record to report. His comparable governing record is executive: he has served as Washington County Executive since 2020, but I did not find a public roll-call voting record in the sources reviewed. Sources: https://www.washcowisco.gov/departments/county_executive ; https://ballotpedia.org/Josh_Schoemann_(Washington_County_Executive,_Wisconsin,_candidate_2024)

links.title: Josh Schoemann - Wisconsin Candidate - Transparency USA
Josh Schoemann on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race
Republican Josh Schoemann to join 2026 governor race

Economy.stance: Schoemann favors tax relief to spur growth: flatten and eventually eliminate the top state income tax bracket and cut property taxes, aiming for a roughly 50% reduction in overall burden by consolidating tax districts. He also advocates a growth‑oriented approach to data centers and energy policy, including pursuing nuclear energy to support affordable, reliable power for Wisconsin’s economy.

sources.title: Josh Schoemann on issues in Wisconsin’s 2026 governor’s race - PBS Wisconsin
Gubernatorial candidate Josh Schoemann plans to focus on taxes - WKOW
2026 GOP candidate Schoemann challenges Evers’ budget approach - The Center Square

Reproductive Rights.stance: Schoemann advocates protecting reproductive rights and ensuring access to health care services for all Wisconsinites.

Immigration.stance: Schoemann has said he supports strong immigration enforcement and cooperation with federal authorities, including ICE, as part of a law-and-order approach to the issue.

sources.title: Washington County Executive Supports ICE Amid Controversy
Interviews with Josh Schoemann - PBS Wisconsin

Guns & Safety.stance: Schoemann says Wisconsin should take a tougher-on-crime approach and give law enforcement, district attorneys, and police departments more resources. In the sources reviewed, he did not outline a detailed gun policy position.

sources.title: Josh Schoemann on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Foreign Policy.stance: Schoemann said he views tariffs as a tool to shape the global economy in a way that benefits American workers and the Western Hemisphere, and he expressed cautious support for President Trump’s tariff approach. He did not lay out a broader foreign policy platform in the sources reviewed.

sources.title: Josh Schoemann on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Education.stance: Josh Schoemann supports universal school choice for every parent and reform of Wisconsin's public-school funding formula dating to the 1970s to improve student outcomes; he calls for bringing together school boards, superintendents, parents, homeschoolers, and voucher schools to design funding and policy that center on student results.

sources.title: Josh Schoemann on issues in Wisconsin's 2026 governor's race

Tech & AI.stance: Schoemann supports targeted AI regulation to protect consumers and workers while avoiding unnecessary barriers to innovation; he also advocates studying AI’s impact on Wisconsin’s workforce and investing in retraining, and views data centers as an opportunity that should be pursued with strategic siting and reliable power (including consideration of nuclear energy) to meet demand.

sources.title: Wisconsin gubernatorial candidates discuss Trump, data centers, AI and marijuana at first forum

Election Reform.stance: Schoemann’s plan for election reform centers on a comprehensive overhaul: abolish the Wisconsin Elections Commission, move all elections to November, end ballot drop boxes and central-count facilities, audit and modernize the system, and shift election administration to elected local officials (clerks) to increase accountability and turnout.

Healthcare.stance: No public position found on healthcare in the sources reviewed.

sources.title: Washington County Executive Josh Schoemann enters the 2026 Wisconsin governor's race

Climate/Energy.stance: Schoemann says Wisconsin should expand nuclear power as part of a broader energy portfolio and be strategic about siting data centers so growth does not outpace power supply and infrastructure. He frames energy reliability as central to affordability, housing, and economic growth.

Social Justice.stance: No public position found on social justice in the sources reviewed.

sources.title: Washington County Executive Josh Schoemann enters the 2026 Wisconsin governor's race

Local Issues.stance: Schoemann emphasizes local control, shared services, housing development, and property-tax relief. As county executive, he points to Washington County initiatives on transportation, housing, and budget management as examples of his approach to local governance.

Education.stance: Manske says he supports AI-driven education options and greater parental freedom over how children learn, including homeschooling. He also says Wisconsin should invest in workforce training so workers can adapt to technological change.

flags.concern: Two polling entries contain null values for sample_size and percentages. The 2026-02-19 Marquette Law School poll entry (polling[3]) and 2026-02-12 RealClearPolling entry (polling[4]) both show incomplete matchup data with null percentages. While the polling_note at the bottom of the profile warns that some entries 'may be incomplete or placeholder records,' these specific entries appear to be stubs rather than complete data points.
flags.suggestion: Either complete these polling entries with actual data from the source URLs, or remove them entirely if the data cannot be retrieved. Alternatively, clarify in the polling_note whether these are intentional placeholder records.

flags.concern: The top_donors array for Sara Rodriguez pulls from an OpenSecrets page labeled 'Tony Evers Top Contributors' (2022 ticket data) rather than from Rodriguez's own 2026 governor campaign finance filings. The profile's donor_summary correctly notes this source issue and explains that OpenSecrets lists 'Sara Rodriguez for Wisconsin' as a contributor to Evers' 2022 committee. However, the top_donors amounts ($13.3M, $120K, $86K, etc.) appear to be aggregated or misattributed, particularly the $13.3M 'Wisconsin Democratic Party' entry, which is unlikely to be a donor to Rodriguez's individual campaign. The profile's donor_summary makes this caveat explicit, but the top_donors array itself may mislead readers who do not read the summary note.
flags.suggestion: Either relocate this data to a separate 'note' field or remove these top_donors entries in favor of a statement that says 'No detailed donor breakdown available in sources reviewed; see Wisconsin campaign finance portal at [URL]' with a link to state-level filings.

flags.concern: Tom Tiffany's top_donors array lists only 'Wisconsin Democratic Party' with $90,000 from an Evers OpenSecrets page (2023 cycle), which appears to be ticket-level data rather than Tiffany's own federal or state campaign contributions. This is incongruous with a Republican candidate and likely reflects the same source attribution error as Rodriguez's profile. The profile notes Tiffany's prior fundraising in the donor_summary ('about $2 million in the governor's race') but provides no itemized donor breakdown.
flags.suggestion: Remove this entry and replace with a reference to OpenSecrets federal campaign data or Wisconsin state finance portal, noting that a detailed donor breakdown was not available in the sources reviewed.

flags.concern: Francesca Hong's voting_record field is present in the profile structure but the actual voting record detail is absent from the candidates[5] object. The voting_summary correctly notes that she is an incumbent Assembly member and has a voting record, and it cites Vote Smart and Ballotpedia, but no specific bills or votes are listed (in contrast to, e.g., Tom Tiffany's voting_record array which includes H.R. 3937). This is not necessarily an error, since the agent may have found no publicly accessible roll-call details, but it is incomplete relative to what is available for other legislators.
flags.suggestion: Either populate voting_record with at least a few recent votes from Vote Smart or the Wisconsin Legislature website (if publicly available), or explicitly note in voting_record (e.g., with a 'no detailed roll-call data found' entry) to indicate that a search was conducted.

flags.concern: Mandela Barnes' Healthcare and Reproductive Rights sections are relatively brief and lack policy detail compared to other Democratic candidates. For example, the Healthcare stance mentions BadgerCare expansion but does not address public option, prescription drug pricing, or other details available for Sara Rodriguez or Kelda Roys. The Reproductive Rights section cites only one source (PBS Wisconsin on abortion laws). While this may reflect genuine gaps in Barnes' public statements, the sourcing is lighter than for other major candidates.
flags.suggestion: If additional policy detail exists in Barnes' campaign materials, add it. Alternatively, note in the voting_summary or issues section that Barnes has emphasized fewer specific policy planks on these issues compared with other primary candidates.

flags.concern: Mike Kohn's issues section shows 'No public position found' with 'low' confidence across all 12 issue areas. While this may be accurate for a minor independent candidate, the lack of any campaign website or social media beyond a Wisconsin campaign finance registration suggests the research agent may not have conducted a thorough search or that Kohn has minimal public profile. The sources cited (Ballotpedia, WUWM, campaign finance portal) would not necessarily capture detailed policy if Kohn has not made public statements.
flags.suggestion: If a campaign website or social media exists, retrieve and cite it. If Kohn genuinely has no public platform, consider whether this candidate merits inclusion in the profile or if a note should be added explaining why.

flags.concern: David D. King's image_url is null, whereas most other candidates have a Wikimedia Commons image URL. This is a minor completeness gap and may reflect absence of a public image in Wikimedia, but it makes the profile less visually complete.
flags.suggestion: Search for a campaign photo on kingforgovernor.com or other public sources and add if found.

flags.concern: Francesca Hong's website field is null, though her campaign site (francescahong.com) is cited multiple times in the issues section and in the links array. This appears to be an oversight in profile compilation rather than a sourcing error.
flags.suggestion: Populate the website field with 'https://francescahong.com/' for consistency and user navigation.

flags.concern: Francesca Hong's career_history array shows only her current State Assembly position (2021–present) and omits her prior work as a chef, restaurant worker, and community organizer, all of which are mentioned in her summary. Similarly, Mandela Barnes' career_history is empty despite his background as Wisconsin's 45th lieutenant governor (2019–2023) being mentioned in the summary and voting_summary. These omissions reduce the completeness of the profile structure.
flags.suggestion: Add prior roles to career_history arrays. For Hong, include her chef/restaurant worker background and community organizing. For Barnes, include his lieutenant governor term with start_year: 2019, end_year: 2023.

flags.concern: Kelda Roys' education array is empty, though her Ballotpedia and official biographies may contain education information. Other candidates in the profile (e.g., Rod Crowley, David D. King) have education sections populated.
flags.suggestion: Retrieve education data from Ballotpedia or official sources if available.

reviews.summary: This is a comprehensive and well-sourced profile of Wisconsin's 2026 gubernatorial race. The profile demonstrates strong sourcing across multiple candidates, with consistent use of official campaign sites, credible news outlets (PBS Wisconsin, Milwaukee Journal Sentinel, Wisconsin Examiner), legislative records, and campaign finance databases. Candidate backgrounds are documented with appropriate hedging where information is incomplete. Issue stances are clearly attributed to campaign materials or news reporting. The profile appropriately documents gaps in public information (e.g., 'no public position found') rather than fabricating positions. Neutrality is generally maintained across both Democratic and Republican candidates, with language reflecting candidate claims and reporting rather than editorial judgment. Minor areas for improvement relate to incomplete polling data and a few candidates with sparse public records.

flags.concern: The donor list for Republican Tom Tiffany appears to be a data-mapping error; it cites an OpenSecrets URL for the Democratic incumbent Tony Evers and lists the 'Wisconsin Democratic Party' as the top contributor. This is internally inconsistent with the candidate's party affiliation.
flags.suggestion: Verify and replace with Tom Tiffany's specific state-level campaign finance filings or his federal OpenSecrets profile (N00045307).

flags.concern: The donor list for Sara Rodriguez lists the total amounts for the Tony Evers 2022 ticket. While she was on this ticket, the $13M figure reflects the governor's committee rather than her individual fundraising for the 2026 cycle.
flags.suggestion: Distinguish between ticket-level historical data and candidate-specific 2026 fundraising as it becomes available.

flags.concern: The image URL for Mike Kohn (Independent) appears to point to a Wikimedia file for an individual of the same name who is a well-known Olympic athlete (bobsledder). It is unclear if this is the same Mike Kohn running for governor.
flags.suggestion: Manually verify if the candidate is the former Olympian or a different individual with a similar name.

flags.concern: The polling entries for 2026-03-24 and 2026-03-18 cite the same URL and contain identical data for 'Sara Rodriguez favorability'.
flags.suggestion: Consolidate these entries or verify if they represent distinct waves of the same Marquette Law School study.

reviews.summary: This profile provides an exceptionally high-quality and comprehensive overview of a wide field of candidates. The research agent has done a stellar job distinguishing between various Democratic and Republican platforms, particularly on nuanced local issues like the moratorium on data centers and the 'KeldaCare' proposal. The issue stances are multi-sourced and maintain a strictly neutral tone throughout.

flags.concern: The source URL primarily references Tony Evers' contributors, which the profile notes may not fully align with Sara Rodriguez's donor data; this could indicate a potential mismatch or indirect application, though the profile's donor_summary provides clarification.
flags.suggestion: Cross-reference with direct campaign finance records for Rodriguez to ensure accuracy.

reviews.summary: This profile is a well-constructed and balanced overview of the 2026 Wisconsin gubernatorial race, with strong sourcing from credible outlets like official campaign sites, Ballotpedia, and reputable news organizations such as PBS Wisconsin and the Milwaukee Journal Sentinel. It maintains neutrality in describing candidate stances and backgrounds, effectively documents gaps for less prominent candidates, and ensures internal consistency by tying claims directly to cited sources, making it a reliable resource for understanding the election dynamics.

polling_note: Some polling entries in this race may be incomplete or placeholder records; verify sample sizes and percentages against the cited source before using them.

validation_grade.summary: Validated by 3/3 reviewers with an average score of 90/100.
